growth_records.medium: In planta symbiotic condition — bacteroid inside root nodule of wild soybean Glycine soja W05 (no defined culture medium; constraint-based model iCC541)

up.method: model input constraint (FBA upper bound), value cited from Udvardi et al. 1988 FEBS Lett 231:36-40
model input constraint (FBA upper bound), value cited from Udvardi et al. 1988 FEBS Lett 231:36-40
model input constraint (FBA upper bound), value cited from Bergersen 1997 Soil Biol Biochem 29:875-880

sec.compound: ammonia (NH3, fixed nitrogen transported to host)
sec.method: FBA model prediction (bounds inferred from W05 transcriptome via E-Flux)

growth_records.cite: Contador CA et al. Metabolic Analyses of Nitrogen Fixation in the Soybean Microsymbiont Sinorhizobium fredii Using Constraint-Based Modeling. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00516-19. [PMC7029217]
growth_records.snippet: Uptake rates of C4-dicarboxylate acids supplied by the host plant, i.e., malate and succinate, were set to 1.44 mmol g-1 (dry weight) h-1 and 1.38 mmol g-1 (dry weight) h-1, respectively. Oxygen consumption rate was limited to 1.26 mmol g-1
growth_records.notes: Constraint-based (FBA) study of the S. fredii CCBAU45436 bacteroid (model iCC541); NO free-living culture and NO measured growth rate. All uptake values are MODEL INPUT CONSTRAINTS, not measured here: malate 1.44 and succinate 1.38 mmol gDW-1 h-1 cited from Udvardi et al. 1988 (FEBS Lett 231:36-40); O2 1.26 mmol gDW-1 h-1 cited from Bergersen 1997 (Soil Biol Biochem 29:875-880). Ammonia secretion 0.0038 mmol gDW-1 h-1 is an FBA prediction for the W05 (wild soybean) symbiosis with flux bounds inferred from CCBAU45436 nodule transcriptome via the E-Flux method. Symbiosis-product-per-mmol-carbon yield for W05 = 0.0032 g/mmol (Table 5). All is_experimental=false. temperature/pH not applicable (in-planta model). culture_mode left blank: none of the allowed enum values (batch/fed-batch/chemostat/turbidostat/retentostat/single-cell/co-culture) applies to an in-planta symbiotic bacteroid FBA simulation.

growth_records.medium: In planta symbiotic condition — bacteroid inside root nodule of cultivated soybean Glycine max C08 (no defined culture medium; constraint-based model iCC541)

up.method: model input constraint (FBA upper bound), value cited from Udvardi et al. 1988 FEBS Lett 231:36-40
model input constraint (FBA upper bound), value cited from Udvardi et al. 1988 FEBS Lett 231:36-40
model input constraint (FBA upper bound), value cited from Bergersen 1997 Soil Biol Biochem 29:875-880

sec.compound: ammonia (NH3, fixed nitrogen transported to host)
sec.method: FBA model prediction (bounds inferred from C08 transcriptome via E-Flux)

growth_records.cite: Contador CA et al. Metabolic Analyses of Nitrogen Fixation in the Soybean Microsymbiont Sinorhizobium fredii Using Constraint-Based Modeling. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00516-19. [PMC7029217]
growth_records.snippet: 0.0032 and 0.0097 g symbiosis product per mmol of carbon uptake were obtained in the symbiosis with W05 and C08, respectively. Additionally, there was a difference in the flux of fixed ammonia with 0.0038 and 0.0275 mmol g-1 (dry weight) h-
growth_records.notes: Companion condition to the W05 record. Same imposed uptake constraints (malate 1.44, succinate 1.38, O2 1.26 mmol gDW-1 h-1; cited from Udvardi 1988 and Bergersen 1997, not measured here). Predicted ammonia secretion for the cultivated-soybean C08 symbiosis = 0.0275 mmol gDW-1 h-1 (FBA with C08 nodule transcriptome bounds via E-Flux), ~7.2x higher than W05, consistent with the paper's conclusion of higher N-fixation capacity in cultivated vs wild soybean. Symbiosis-product-per-mmol-carbon yield for C08 = 0.0097 g/mmol (Table 5). No free-living culture, no measured growth rate; all is_experimental=false. culture_mode left blank (no applicable enum value for in-planta bacteroid FBA).

growth_records.medium: In silico minimal medium (free-living FBA constraints); model iAQY970

up.method: In-silico FBA medium constraint, cited as confirmed by Contador et al., 2020 (iCC541); not measured in this study

sec.method: FBA model output (iAQY970), symbiotic case; compared to iCC541 0.0487

growth_records.cite: Ye, Anqiang et al. Reconstruction of the genome-scale metabolic network model of Sinorhizobium fredii CCBAU45436 for free-living and symbiotic states. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2024.1377334. [PMC10999553]
growth_records.snippet: In silico media compositions were 1.44 mmol g−1 h−1 malate, 1.38 mmol g−1 h−1 succinate, 1.26 mmol g−1 h−1 oxygen, 2 mmol g−1 h−1 glutamate and 0.01 mmol g−1 h−1 inositol, which were confirmed by previous literature (Contador et al., 2020).
growth_records.notes: GEM reconstruction paper (iAQY970, MEMOTE 89%, growth-phenotype 81.7% consistent). NO experimental quantitative prokaryote rate measured in this study. The uptake rates (malate 1.44, succinate 1.38, O2 1.26, glutamate 2, inositol 0.01 mmol/gDW/h) are IN-SILICO FBA medium constraints explicitly cited as confirmed by Contador et al., 2020 (iCC541) — is_experimental=false. Symbiotic uptake/production and ATP/NAD(P)H rates in Table 2 are FBA model outputs, not measurements. The only wet-lab data here is Biolog GEN III qualitative carbon-source utilization (43/71 sources usable), which gives no quantitative rates. Record retained for GEM-constraint provenance only; flagged non-experimental.

growth_records.medium: RMS (rhizobia minimal medium supplemented with succinate)

up.method: not measured (sole carbon source; no consumption rate reported)

growth_records.cite: Cherni, Ala Eddine et al. Deletion of rRNA Operons of Sinorhizobium fredii Strain NGR234 and Impact on Symbiosis With Legumes. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00154. [PMC6381291]
growth_records.snippet: generation time for each of the tested strains was estimated at 160 (+/-10) min for NGR234 ... cells were grown in RMS liquid cultures at 27C
growth_records.notes: Generation (doubling) time REPORTED = 160 +/-10 min = 2.6667 h (estimated from exponential phase of OD600 growth curves, Fig 2, 3 replicate cultures). mu CALCULATED: mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/2.6667 = 0.2599 /h. RMS = rhizobia minimal medium + succinate as sole C source (composition cited from Broughton et al. 1986, not given in this paper). No uptake or secretion rates measured. Also grown on TY rich medium and on RMS/TY agar (colony diameters) but liquid RMS growth curve gives the rate.

growth_records.strain: NGRdeltarRNA3 (rRNA3 operon deleted, Sp-R Omega cassette; two functional rRNA operons)
growth_records.medium: RMS (rhizobia minimal medium supplemented with succinate)
growth_records.cite: Cherni, Ala Eddine et al. Deletion of rRNA Operons of Sinorhizobium fredii Strain NGR234 and Impact on Symbiosis With Legumes. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00154. [PMC6381291]
growth_records.notes: Doubling time REPORTED = 185 +/-14 min = 3.0833 h (OD600 growth curve, liquid RMS, 27C). mu CALCULATED = ln2/3.0833 = 0.2248 /h. Mutant with two of three rRNA operons. No uptake/secretion rates.

growth_records.strain: NGRdeltarRNA1 (rRNA1 operon deleted, Km-R Omega cassette; two functional rRNA operons)
growth_records.medium: RMS (rhizobia minimal medium supplemented with succinate)
growth_records.cite: Cherni, Ala Eddine et al. Deletion of rRNA Operons of Sinorhizobium fredii Strain NGR234 and Impact on Symbiosis With Legumes. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00154. [PMC6381291]
growth_records.notes: Doubling time REPORTED = 190 +/-16 min = 3.1667 h (liquid RMS OD600 curve, 27C). mu CALCULATED = ln2/3.1667 = 0.2189 /h. Mutant with two of three rRNA operons. No uptake/secretion rates.

growth_records.strain: NGRdeltarRNA1,3 (double mutant; single remaining rRNA2 operon)
growth_records.medium: RMS (rhizobia minimal medium supplemented with succinate)
growth_records.cite: Cherni, Ala Eddine et al. Deletion of rRNA Operons of Sinorhizobium fredii Strain NGR234 and Impact on Symbiosis With Legumes. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00154. [PMC6381291]
growth_records.snippet: doubling time of NGRDeltarRNA1,3 was ... considerably extended ... as much as 245 (+/-8 min) for NGRDeltarRNA1,3
growth_records.notes: Doubling time REPORTED = 245 +/-8 min = 4.0833 h (liquid RMS OD600 curve, 27C). mu CALCULATED = ln2/4.0833 = 0.1698 /h. Double mutant leaving a single rRNA operon; grows significantly slower than WT. No uptake/secretion rates.
